commands.command: thanks for the careful review. both p1 and p2 findings were correct - addressed in 11d022974a. **p1 - fenced code block preservation** the normalizer now walks the text fence-by-fence (recognising triple-backtick and triple-tilde fences with any info string) and only applies hr strip + list blank-line collapse to prose segments. code blocks - including yaml frontmatter, config snippets, or anything that legitimately contains a `---` line - pass through verbatim. an unclosed fence treats the tail as code (defensive: do not rewrite content the user might be reading mid-truncation). 6 new outbound-format.test.ts cases pin the contract: - `---` line inside triple-backtick fence preserved - `---` line inside triple-tilde fence preserved - blank-lined "list" inside a fence preserved - 4+ backtick fences recognised - prose around a fence still normalizes; fence content stays intact - unclosed fence does not normalize the tail **p2 - textstyles offset safety** `sendmessagezalouser` now skips normalization entirely when `textmode !== "markdown"` and `options.textstyles` is non-empty. markdown mode is unaffected because `parsezalousertextstyles` re-derives style ranges from the post-normalization text. added an integration test in send.test.ts asserting that a plain-mode send with caller-supplied styles reaches the transport with the original text + original style array verbatim. **real behavior proof** you are right that i removed the "verified live deployment" line from the pr body - my contributor account cannot attach a redacted zalo client screenshot from the inside, and i should not have made that claim without an attached artifact. the normalizer was first prototyped as a vendor patch in a downstream deployment where it visibly fixed the rendering, but the proof for upstream review needs to come from a maintainer with their own zalo test account. the unit + integration tests cover the contract; the visual outcome is best confirmed by a maintainer running the build against a real zalo client. full zalouser suite: 185 passed, 2 skipped (unrelated). ready for re-review.

Both P1 and P2 findings were correct - addressed in 11d022974a. **P1 - fenced code block preservation** The normalizer now walks the text fence-by-fence (recognising triple-backtick and triple-tilde fences with any info string) and only applies HR strip + list blank-line collapse to prose segments. Code blocks - including YAML frontmatter, config snippets, or anything that legitimately contains a `---` line - pass through verbatim. An unclosed fence treats the tail as code (defensive: do not rewrite content the user might be reading mid-truncation). 6 new outbound-format.test.ts cases pin the contract: - `---` line inside triple-backtick fence preserved - `---` line inside triple-tilde fence preserved - Blank-lined "list" inside a fence preserved - 4+ backtick fences recognised - Prose around a fence still normalizes; fence content stays intact - Unclosed fence does not normalize the tail **P2 - textStyles offset safety** `sendMessageZalouser` now skips normalization entirely when `textMode !== "markdown"` AND `options.textStyles` is non-empty. Markdown mode is unaffected because `parseZalouserTextStyles` re-derives style ranges from the post-normalization text. Added an integration test in send.test.ts asserting that a plain-mode send with caller-supplied styles reaches the transport with the original text + original style array verbatim. **Real behavior proof** You are right that I removed the "verified live deployment" line from the PR body - my contributor account cannot attach a redacted Zalo client screenshot from the inside, and I should not have made that claim without an attached artifact. target.title: feat(zalouser): normalize agent-emitted markdown for Zalo client rendering
target.body:
## Problem

The Zalo personal-account client (mobile + web) renders agent-style markdown poorly in two specific ways that the zalouser plugin currently passes through unchanged:

1. **Triple-dash / triple-asterisk horizontal rules** (`---`, `***`) render as literal dashes / asterisks on their own line - never as a separator. LLM agents emit them as section dividers; recipients see them as garbage.

2. **List items separated by blank lines** render with the blank line preserved, breaking the visual grouping a reader expects from a markdown list. `- foo\n- bar` reads as one list; `- foo\n\n- bar` reads as two unrelated items with extra vertical space. LLM agents commonly emit lists this way because Slack / Discord / the web playground render that style best - Zalo is the outlier.

Both surface to deployments where the message a user receives looks broken even though the model output is well-formed markdown.

## Solution

Add a `normalizeZalouserOutboundText` pre-processor invoked at the top of `sendMessageZalouser`. The transform is conservative and **block-aware**: it walks the text fence-by-fence and only applies HR strip + list blank-line collapse to prose segments. Content inside ``` / ~~~ fenced code blocks (YAML frontmatter, config snippets, anything containing a `---` line) passes through verbatim.

It is also idempotent (only removes characters, never adds), so applying it twice is identical to once.

**Behavior on prose segments:**
- Strips lines that are nothing but 3+ dashes / asterisks (with optional leading / trailing whitespace). Leaves surrounding newlines so adjacent paragraphs keep their blank-line break.
- Collapses 1+ blank lines BETWEEN, BEFORE, or AFTER list items (`-`, `*`, `1.`) down to a single newline so the list reads as one block.
- Hard cap of 2 consecutive newlines anywhere else (defensive).

**On fenced segments:**
- Triple-backtick and triple-tilde fences (any length, any info string) recognised.
- Content passes through unmodified.
- An unclosed fence treats the tail as code - defensive against mid-truncation cases.

**On caller-supplied textStyles in plain mode:**
- `sendMessageZalouser` skips normalization entirely when `textMode !== "markdown"` AND `options.textStyles` is non-empty. Those style offsets describe the original text and the transport layer only clamps, never rebases. Markdown mode is unaffected because `parseZalouserTextStyles` re-derives styles from the post-normalization text.

**Why a pre-processor, not a renderer change:** the existing `parseZalouserTextStyles` path is concerned with Zalo's native text-style payload (bold, italic, etc.), not with the structural cleanup the client needs. Splitting the concern keeps `text-styles` focused and lets the normalizer run regardless of `textMode`.

## Tests

**`outbound-format.test.ts`** - 25 cases covering:
- Identity / passthrough (empty, plain prose, non-string defensive)
- HR stripping (positive: dashes, asterisks, longer runs, leading / trailing whitespace; negative: mixed-content line, two-dash that is not an HR)
- Inter-item blank-line collapse for bullet, asterisk, and numbered lists, and the mixed case
- Edge cases at list boundaries (blank lines between prose and first item, last item and following prose)
- Hard newline cap
- Fenced code block preservation: triple-backtick + triple-tilde, longer fences, lists-inside-fence, prose-around-fence, unclosed fence
- Idempotency property assertion on a representative agent reply

**`send.test.ts`** - 3 new integration cases:
- End-to-end normalization on a list-with-blanks reaches the transport with the tight form
- `---` line inside a code fence reaches the transport intact
- Plain-mode send with caller-supplied `textStyles` skips normalization (offsets stay aligned)

Full zalouser package suite: 185 passed, 2 skipped (unrelated).

## Related

Companion to openclaw/openclaw#84924 (inbound photo support upstream PR). Both came from the same deployment surfacing real rendering gaps in the zalouser plugin.

## Note on visual proof

The normalizer was first prototyped as a vendor patch in a downstream deployment where it visibly fixed the Zalo client rendering. I cannot attach a redacted Zalo client screenshot from the contributor side - the visual outcome is best confirmed by a maintainer running the build against their own Zalo test account. The unit + integration tests pin the contract (what text shape reaches the transport for each input); the visual rendering of that shape is what a maintainer review can verify.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
